name: Minimal flavor violation (MFV)
summary: An ansatz rather than a UV theory: assume that the only source of flavor and CP violation in any BSM extension is the SM Yukawa matrices themselves, treated as spurions of the broken U(3)^5 quark/lepton flavor symmetry. Predicts that BSM flavor-violating amplitudes are proportional to the same CKM elements that govern SM amplitudes, naturally suppressing FCNCs.

addresses_puzzles.note: Does not predict the Yukawa hierarchy from first principles; it treats the SM Yukawas as inputs and ensures BSM physics does not introduce new flavor-violating sources beyond them. Combined with a mechanism that explains the Yukawa hierarchy itself (FN, modular), MFV is a 'flavor-safe' wrapper.

touches_benchmarks.note: By construction, BSM contributions to K-Kbar, B-Bbar mixing follow CKM hierarchy; consistent with measured rates without additional alignment.
Lepton-sector MFV with the PMNS matrix as the only LFV source; rates are PMNS-suppressed.

new_symmetries: U(3)^5 flavor symmetry (broken only by Yukawa spurions)
introduces.note: MFV is a constraint on BSM model-building, not a new theory. It introduces no new parameters; it only restricts how new fields can couple to flavor.

history: Hall-Randall (1990); Buras et al. (2000); D'Ambrosio, Giudice, Isidori, Strumia (2002) gave the modern formulation as a spurion analysis. Widely used as a bottom-up flavor consistency check in BSM physics.

references.citation: D'Ambrosio, Giudice, Isidori, Strumia, 'Minimal flavor violation: an effective field theory approach', Nucl. Phys. B 645, 155 (2002)
